untranslatable_reason: This signal requires real-time monitoring of three separate instruments (futures lock limit status, call option prices, put option prices) and synthetic position construction across derivatives. The allowed indicator set contains only spot/futures price action and technical indicators—it lacks: (1) option chain data (call/put prices, implied volatility), (2) circuit breaker/limit-up/limit-down status flags, (3) liquidity/trading halted flags, and (4) cross-instrument synthetic arbitrage logic. The signal's core mechanism (using call-put parity to infer a theoretical futures price during a trading halt) cannot be expressed with price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, or Bar Properties alone.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_305 is an event-driven options strategy requiring access to derivatives data, exchange status, and volatility surfaces. It is fundamentally incompatible with a futures-only technical indicator backtest engine. Would require a dedicated options/event processing module with access to option chain feeds and circuit breaker data.
